---
title: "Students & Collaboration"
---

I lead a research group working on demography, life-history evolution, and phenology. To date I have supervised four postdoctoral researchers, four PhD students, and more than thirty MSc graduates, across topics including comparative plant demography, woodland phenology, and mammalian ageing.

I welcome enquiries from prospective students and collaborators whose interests align with these themes.

## Before contacting me

Please read my research overview and send a concise message outlining your specific interests, relevant background, and why there is a good fit with my work.

Include:

- your current role and institution
- a short description of your research interests or project idea
- relevant methods or data experience (for example R, modelling, field/lab systems)
- practical details such as expected timing and funding context (if known)

## Prospective PhD students

Strong enquiries usually include a focused problem, awareness of the relevant literature, and clear motivation for joining this research area. If you are considering doctoral study, it helps to outline one or two well-defined directions rather than a broad generic statement.

## Collaboration enquiries

I am open to collaborations that are methodologically clear and scientifically specific, especially where there is strong complementarity in data, theory, or analytical expertise.

## Email enquiries

Focused, tailored emails are especially helpful. A short, well-prepared message is the best starting point.
